title: Feature Request: Automatic XML Import from URI
author: darkpixel
body_snippet:
I would love to see a feature in mRemote where it would automatically load/import an XML config file on startup.

This would be helpful for provisioning mRemote for lots of users.

An example environment would be a call center where users needs to connect out to various offices to pull up records.

Rather than reminding everyone to "delete their mRemote config and then re-import it from the usual location", I would love to tell mRemote to always automatically load connections from ```https...

iterations.description: Issue body is empty, labeled 'In development' but appears stale with no implementation details or reproducible steps provided.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: what will be posible with profiles implementation, but also its a lot of questions of security for such way - file should be encrypted, and user should have access to it, in my world (where i am working at the moment) we control credentials with cyberark, so user never know password. mremote have only connection details - then call cyberArk for credentials and login into system. On next login same credentials will not work if password or user name (or both) has been changed :)

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
I don't see the direct use case when one could use a database for the connection profiles (current implementation works but requires improvement). The database would dynamically update the settings everywhere without the need of downloading config files on startup. I see this kind of distribution with proxy configurations and IMHO it's quite a pain to maintain...

On the other hand, the ability to import connections with a double click would be great, maybe also allowing for one-time use (like...

comments.author: darkpixel
comments.snippet:
I don't want to:
1. Provision a database in the cloud (why spend $15/mo?) and make it accessible from everywhere on the internet as users roam all over the place and don't have static IPs.
2. Walk users through picking "mysql" from the dropdown, entering "mremote-connections-do-user-134425-0.b.db.ondigitalocean.com" in the hostname, entering a database name, username, password, and checking the 'read only' button.
3. Spend 15 minutes telling each of 300 users the database password is 'tathie6...
